title: OAuthStore has no expiry handling, no auto-refresh, pending_challenges leak memory
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

Dimension: Security and Access / Architectural Alignment

Location: src/oauth.rs lines 289-306, 322-329, 332-350 (OAuthStore methods).

Violation: OAuthStore provides no explicit session expiry / eviction logic during runtime:
  - store_session: inserts with no TTL on the in-memory HashMap.
  - get_session (line 322): returns a clone of OAuthSession regardless of its credentials.expires_at. Downstream proxy_anthropic_messages (line 1065) uses `session.credentials.access_token` WITHOUT checking is_expired(). An expired token will be blindly sent to Anthropic, who will return 401, which we then forward to the client as an opaque upstream error.
  - get_any_valid_session (line 332): DOES check is_expired but in a scan over all sessions. If none are valid, returns None — and the code path then falls back to API-key extraction silently (no "your OAuth session expired, please re-authenticate" message).
  - pending_challenges: PKCE challenges are stored indefinitely. A never-completed device-flow challenge leaves an entry in the RwLock<HashMap>. Memory leak vector: a cron that hits /auth/device/start every second fills the map.

Critically: when an access token is expired, OAuthStore does NOT automatically refresh using the stored refresh_token. OAuthClient::refresh_token exists (line 561) but is never called by the store. Users must re-authenticate from scratch every hour.

Mandated Refactor:
  1. Add `async fn get_active_session(&self, id: &str, client: &OAuthClient) -> Option<OAuthSession>` that checks expiry and auto-refreshes using credentials.refresh_token if present (with the refresh_buffer constant from claude_credentials.rs). Persist the refreshed session.
  2. Add a background reaper task: `tokio::spawn` in OAuthStore::new that every 60s removes pending_challenges older than 10 minutes and sessions that are expired AND have no refresh_token.
  3. proxy_anthropic_messages must use get_active_session, not get_session, and propagate refresh failures as 401 Unauthorized with a 'oauth_expired' error code.
  4. /auth/status should return `{authenticated: false, reason: 'token_expired', can_refresh: true}` when the session is expired but refresh_token is present — allowing the CLI to know it just needs to poll for a refresh rather than restart device flow.
